mechanism_narrative: A decline in FEDFUNDS precedes easier discount-rate conditions and portfolio reallocation into equities, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when rate cuts are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns and rejected when SPX weakens despite falling policy rates.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS declines or stays flat but SPX delivers negative 20-day returns in most such episodes, or rolling 2-year Rank IC falls below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent level signals weakening labor income and rising recession risk, which reduces expected earnings and market multiples. When unemployment is accelerating, S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the effect is positive in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSI-like demand weakness shows up as simultaneous declines in RSAFS and HOUST, consumers and housing-linked spending are both softening, which should compress near-term earnings expectations and pressure equities. The joint deterioration matters because either series alone is less informative than the combination.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-low-demand state does not predict lower 20-day SPX returns than either series alone, or if joint-triggered episodes have nonnegative median forward returns over a 3-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: Falling INDPRO alongside stable or rising CPIAUCSL indicates supply constraints rather than pure demand weakness, which supports margin compression and lower equity returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism is stronger when output slows while inflation remains elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with declining INDPRO and firm CPIAUCSL do not underperform over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term is insignificant across successive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in HOUST above trend reflects housing-cycle acceleration and stronger cyclical demand, which improves earnings breadth and supports equity prices over the next 5 trading days. This is a short-horizon risk-on signal only when the housing move is clearly above recent trend.
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend HOUST episodes fail to produce positive 5-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the sign reverses in a rolling 1-year test.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated FEDFUNDS and accelerating CPIAUCSL indicates a tightening/anti-inflation regime that lifts real discount rates and depresses equity valuations over the next 20 trading days. The signal should be strongest when policy rates are high relative to inflation trend.
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation episodes do not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction loses explanatory power after controlling for either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A fall in PAYEMS together with a rising UNRATE captures broad payroll deterioration, which should reduce aggregate income growth and raise recession odds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The joint labor weakness is the operative mechanism, not either series in isolation.
failure_condition: Reject if combined payroll losses and rising unemployment do not lead to lower 60-day SPX returns, or if the hit rate is not materially below 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 is accelerating while RSAFS is also improving, the economy is sustaining real activity and nominal sales growth, which should support earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The signal fails if output growth does not translate into sales growth.
regime_hint: broad expansion with rising nominal demand
failure_condition: Reject if concurrent GDP and retail-sales improvement does not outperform over 60 trading days, or if the interaction term becomes negative in any 3-year rolling out-of-sample window.
